name: Lentil Pheasant Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Parsnip, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch dice
4 cups Defatted canned or fresh chicken broth
1 tbsp Fresh rosemary, chopped OR 1 tsp Dried rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil and butter in a soup pot over medium heat.
2. Add carrots, celery, parsnip, and garlic. Cook, covered, for 15 minutes, stirring once to wilt the vegetables.
3. Stir in chicken broth and cook until heated through.
4. Add lentils, pheasant legs with backbone, and simmer partially covered for 15 minutes.
5. Remove pheasant legs and breasts; set aside. Leave the backbone in the pot.
6. Add chopped parsley, rosemary (or dried), allspice, pepper, and salt. Stir well.
7. Simmer, covered, for another 20 minutes.
8. While the soup simmers, remove skin from pheasant and shred the meat.
9. Once the soup is ready, add shredded pheasant pieces back into the pot along with sherry and remaining parsley.
10. Serve immediately.
